Skip The Arc

The Arc

The Arc

The ARC:
Need Support with your Academics? Want to meet with an ARC Specialist?
The Academic Resource Center (ARC) strives towards the success of Westcliff students by providing quality learning assistance and academic support, both online and in-person. The ARC provides students the opportunity to meet with a Writing or Content Specialist, use a computer to work on assignments, and to simply have a quiet space to study. 
 
ON CAMPUS AND ONLINE LINK, WITH HOURS: https://westcliff.mywconline.com (*new users will need to create an account using their Westcliff email)
 
 We encourage all students to make appointments or email the arc@westcliff.edu with any academic related questions.

      General

      Collapse all Expand all

      WEB 401/WEB 601 Front-End Web Development

      Meeting Day: Monday, Tuesday, Thursday

      Meeting Time: 1:40 PM - 4:40 PM PST

      Location: Irvine Weekday

      Professor: Elnaz Khatmi

      Email: elnazkhatmi@westcliff.edu

      Teaching Assistant 1: 

      Teaching Assistant 2: 

      Teaching Assistant 3: 

      Slack Channel:

      • Acknowledgement of Course Expectations Agreement (Required) Questionnaire
        You must
        Done:
        Student must submit this questionnaire to complete it

        ***ATTENTION STUDENTS***

        Please acknowledge that you have read the Course Expectations Agreement and will comply with the requirements by completing this questionnaire. 

        If you do not complete the questionnaire, you will not be able to access any of the weekly assignments and materials until the questionnaire is complete.

        Course Expectations Agreement

    • Collapse Expand

      Before Your Course Begins: Start Here for Success

      Hello Students and Welcome!
      Before you begin the course please take the time to review the course documents and add a brief biography.



      • Applications Requirement

      • Computer Requirements Page
      • Browser Installation and Updates Page
      • VS Code Installation File
      • Zoom Installation Page

      • Career Preparation



        Career Preparation consists of preparing your career assets and training for the job search. 

      • Westcliff University Bootcamp Career Toolkit Book
      • Lesson 1: Online Presence
      • Lesson 2: Professional Resume/CV

      4 May - 10 May

      Week 1

      • Wk1 Day1 Participation/VCS Submission Assignment
        You must
        To do:

      • Wk1 Day2 Participation/VCS Submission Assignment

      • Assignment Links:

      • BEFORE COURSE BEGINS

      • DAY 1


      • Week1: Class 1 - lesson plan File

        Students should review the Week One Lesson Plan.

        • Overview of Learning Objectives
        • Allows planning of time
        • Enhances organization

      • Week 1: Lesson 1 - HTML Foundation File

        Students will be introduced to;

        • web service fundamentals, 
        • HTML, and 
        • fundamental concepts of creating a web page. 
        This includes; 

        • structuring body elements, 
        • inserting anchor links and multimedia (imagery/graphics), and 
        • how to write HTML comments.

      • In-Class Activity


      • Week 1: Code-Along In-Class Exercise 1 File

        In-class Exercise

        • Students will code along with the instructor during class. 
        • Please review this exercise before your first class.

      • Week 1: Code-Along Lesson 1a - GitHub File


        A step-by-step Guided Learning to build a simple website and commit to GitHub.

        • Students will code along with the instructor during class. 
        • Please review this exercise before your first class.

      • GitHub: Git Cheat Sheet File

        Git is the open source distributed version control system that facilitates GitHub activities on your laptop or desktop. 

        • This cheat sheet summarizes commonly used Git command line instructions for quick reference.

      • End of Day 1 Demo Exercise File

      • Homework

      • Week 1: Homework 1 File
      • week 1 Day 1 Homework 1 Text Content (content-w1d1.txt) File

        This file contains the text required to complete, and mentioned in the Week 1 Homework 1.

      • End of Day 1 Homework File

        This is an illustration of what the completed index.html file will look like when rendered in the web browser.

      • DAY 2

      • Week1: Class 2 - lesson plan File

        • Overview of Learning Objectives
        • Allows planning of time

      • Week 1: Lesson 2 - HTML Essentials File

        • Students will be introduced to the concepts of Media Elements, HTML Tables, and Forms.

      • In-Class Activity

      • IN-CLASS DEMO

      • Module1 week1 Day2 Exercise File

        In-class Code-Along exercise is to be worked on within the class. 

        • Please review this exercise before your class starts.

      • images File
      • week1-day1 (finished version from day1) File

      • Homework

      • Module1 week1 Day2 Homework File

        You will continue from the previous session’s (Day 1) homework. 

        • Just like the demo exercises, you will add two more pages and on each page, 
        • you will add a data table and a contact form on the respective pages. 
          • See the finish examples on GAP.

      • content-w1d2 File
      • images File

      • Dropboxes

      • Week 1 Day 2 Code-Along Exercise Assignment

        Due: Today 10.30PM PT.  Post Github URL Link

      • Week 1 Day 2 Homework Assignment

        Due: Sunday 10.30PM PT.  Post Github URL Link

      • DAY 3 (Lab Day)


      • Week's Lesson Review

      • Week 1: Lesson 3 - Review File

        Review 

        • Setting up a HTML page with basic HTML structure
        • headings 
        • paragraphs
        • lists
        • text links
        • images
        • tables 
        • forms

      • Lab Assignment

      • Week 1: Lab Assignment File

        Create a mini site which consists of 2 html pages

        • Add headings, paragraphs, lists, text links, images and a table.
        • You will be responsible for researching for contents. 
          • Recommended source of research for information: Wikipedia.
        • Site Topic: Your Local Community Board or Local Pet Adoption Service.

      • lab-assignment-topics-examples File

      • Homework

      • Week 1 Class 3 - Homework File

        Add third page to this Mini Site Lab Assignment
        • Add a form on the page.
        • Link all 3 pages.
        • Push changes to GitHub Repo created for Lab

      • Quiz

        Opens Saturday 9AM PT. Close next day Sunday at 11.59PM PT.

      • Quiz - Week 1
        TAKE THE QUIZ AT THE END OF THIS LAB SESSION (WEEK 1)

        • You have 3 attempts, and 30 minutes for this quiz each attempt.
        • 10 Questions

        Please retake the quiz until you earn 100%.

        • Repetition enhances learning
        • Increases your overall grade

      • Assignment Links:

      • Day 1

        Beginning this week, we will transition to the new class format. Here are the activities:

        1. Pre-Reading: you are required to read the lesson slides provided before coming to class.
        2. Announcements/Updates
        3. Homework Review / Code Sharing
        4. Pop Quiz
        5. In-Class Code Along Demo Exercise
        6. Group Challenge
        7. Homework

      • Pre-Reading

        Agenda
        • CSS Basics
        • Inline vs. Embed vs. External Styles
        • How it works
        • CSS Anatomy
        • CSS Selectors: elements, id, class
        • CSS Comments
        • CSS Properties

      18 May - 24 May

      Current week

      Week 3

      • Wk3 Day1 Participation/VCS Submission Assignment
        You must

      • Wk3 Day3 Participation/VCS Submission Assignment
        You must
        To do:
        Make a submission


      • Assignment Links:

      • DAY 1


      • Pre-Class Reading

         JavaScript Foundations

        AGENDA:

        • The methods of writing JavaScript: Internal vs External
        • What are variables & how to create and use them
        • JavaScript data types 
        • What are functions & how to create and use them
        • What are operators and their meanings 
        • What is Decision Making and the use of if-else if-else and switch statements
        • Test codes using the browser console 

      • Module1 week3 Day1 Lesson File

      • In-Class Activities

      • CODE ALONG DEMO

        Using Chrome browser’s JavaScript Console, we will practice Javascript concepts you'd learned today.

      • Module1 week3 Day1 Exercise File

      • GROUP CHALLENGE

      • Homework

        In this homework, you will add a Javascript digital clock on the home page of the Great Cliff website.

      • DAY 2


      • Pre-Class Reading

         JavaScript Foundations

        • What are loops, its construct and use
        • What are arrays, its construct and use
        • The purpose of popup boxes, its construct and use
        • Test codes using the browser console 

      • Module1 week3 Day2 Lesson File

        Review of Javascript

        • What are variables & how to create and use them
        • JavaScript data types

        • What are operators and their meanings

        • The purpose of popup boxes, its construct and use
